Home»Sport»Paris Olympics 2024»Meet the real Tatjana: Team SA’s Golden Girl at the Paris 2024 Olympics

Some fascinating insights here.

Tatjana Smith has been a household name around South Africa for a while. However, her antics at the Paris 2024 Olympics have taken things up a notch. Did you know she missed qualification for the 200m breaststroke at Rio 2016 by 0.01 seconds?

Info viaolympics.com’sofficial website.

A personal highlight for Tanja was winning 200m breaststroke gold at the 2018 Commonwealth Games on the Gold Coast of Australia.

“Having my parents in the stands with some of our family friends and winning my first major competition.”

“My love for swimming makes me want to get up every morning and go to training. It’s like my second home. Being fortunate enough to travel the world doing what I love, seeing new places and meeting new people and sports heroes, motivates me a lot. The most important thing every young swimmer should realise is that nothing is impossible. They only need to believe and be prepared to put in the long and hard hours.”

When reflecting on missing out on the 200m breaststroke in Rio, Smith was typically philosophical and upbeat about one of the biggest setbacks in her professional career.

“2016 was a bit of a disappointment, but I knew everything happens for a reason. I wasn’t ready at that time. We got our hopes up and then I kind of got a dip, creating all that expectation. My fall was quite big, and I had to try and fall in love with the sport again.

“Looking back, maybe I needed to go through that disappointment to take me where I was in 2021 – more of a confident swimmer.”

Smithstands to become only the second swimmer in history after Penny Heyns in 1996 to complete the 100m and 200m breaststroke golden double at the same Olympics event. She wasn’t yet born when Heyns accomplished this historic feat. The 27-year-old cruised through her 200m breaststroke heat and has qualified for the semi-final. That will go onWednesday 31 July between 21:51 and 21:58. The finalis onThursday 01 August at 21:04.You know thatNando’s will be watching. Smith won this event in Tokyo with a time of2.18.95, a world record which has since been eclipsed by Russian Evgeniia Chikunova in 2023 with a time of2.17.55.Will she take her record back?

title: Golden Girl at the Paris 2024 Olympics: Meet the REAL Tatjana
author: James Freemantle
description: Tatjana Smith was already a household name in South Africa. However, her antics at the Paris 2024 Olympics have taken things up a notch.
